other_observers: David Cornell and his wife, and one other guy (can't remember his name). This during the CBC.; I guess I'm the original finder.
description: Distinct red caps on all individuals. Slightly smaller than House Finches in same area. Small, sharp, yellow bills with black down chin and across eyes. I see them as having a rather "pinched" expression. Overall much more pale than other finches or sparrows in area. Chest overall white-ish with brown streaks down flanks. Half of individuals had pale red flush on chest. Backs brown mottled.Behaviors: Roosted as a group. Preferred nijer seed to sunflower. They've been at my feeders for about a month now and I'm hearing about them at other homes in the area.Call: I didn't get a good read on the calls. There were too many other birds around.Plumage: I would assume non-breeding plumage.
